title: The woman who waters the office plants
summary: Describe a quiet co-worker whose small job is keeping the office plants alive, honouring unnoticed work.
topic: A co-worker who cares for the office plants
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a person who does a small, quiet job that most people do not notice. Describe what they do, how often, and why it matters even though nobody thanks them. Use present simple, adverbs of frequency, and 'but' to show that the work is small but important.
hintStarters: Every Monday morning, before the meeting, Mrs Tan…
Most people never notice her, but…
Nobody pays her for this, yet she…
sampleText: There is a quiet woman in my office, and her name is Mrs Tan. She works at the front desk, but she also cares for all the plants. Every Monday morning, before the meeting, she walks around with a small green bottle. She waters each pot slowly and checks the soil with one finger. She often cleans the dust off the leaves with a soft cloth. Sometimes she moves a plant to a sunny corner because it looks weak. Most people never notice her, but the office is always green and fresh. Nobody pays her for this, yet she does it every week. The plants by the window grow tall, and one even has small white flowers now. When I feel tired at my desk, I look at the green leaves and I feel calm. Mrs Tan keeps these small living things alive in a room full of computers. Her job is very small, but I think it makes everyone here a little happier. Last week I finally said thank you, and she just smiled.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third-person -s and adverbs of frequency
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Vì chủ ngữ là 'she', động từ thêm -s: 'she waters', 'she checks', 'she cleans', 'she does'. Trạng từ tần suất như 'often' đứng trước động từ chính: 'she often cleans'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Because the subject is 'she', the verb takes -s: 'she waters', 'she checks', 'she cleans', 'she does'. Frequency adverbs like 'often' go before the main verb: 'she often cleans'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'but' to contrast small and important
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'but' để cho thấy hai ý trái nhau: 'Her job is very small, but it makes everyone here a little happier.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'but' to show two opposite ideas: 'Her job is very small, but it makes everyone here a little happier.'

sampleTranslationVi: Có một người phụ nữ kín đáo trong văn phòng của tôi, và bà ấy tên là Mrs Tan. Bà làm việc ở bàn lễ tân, nhưng bà cũng chăm sóc tất cả các cây. Mỗi sáng thứ Hai, trước cuộc họp, bà đi quanh với một cái bình nhỏ màu xanh. Bà tưới từng chậu một cách chậm rãi và kiểm tra đất bằng một ngón tay. Bà thường lau bụi khỏi những chiếc lá bằng một mảnh vải mềm. Đôi khi bà chuyển một cây đến góc có nắng vì nó trông yếu. Hầu hết mọi người không bao giờ để ý đến bà, nhưng văn phòng luôn xanh tươi. Không ai trả tiền cho bà việc này, vậy mà bà làm nó mỗi tuần. Những cây bên cửa sổ mọc cao, và một cây bây giờ thậm chí còn có những bông hoa trắng nhỏ. Khi tôi thấy mệt ở bàn làm việc, tôi nhìn những chiếc lá xanh và tôi thấy bình yên. Mrs Tan giữ cho những sinh vật sống nhỏ bé này còn sống trong một căn phòng đầy máy tính. Công việc của bà rất nhỏ, nhưng tôi nghĩ nó làm cho mọi người ở đây vui hơn một chút. Tuần trước cuối cùng tôi đã nói lời cảm ơn, và bà chỉ mỉm cười.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best introduces what Mrs Tan also does?
options: She works at the front desk, but she also care for all the plants.
She works at the front desk, but she also cares for all the plants.
She work at the front desk, but she also cares for all the plants.
She works at the front desk, but for all the plants she also cares it.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs agree with 'she' (works, cares), and 'but' adds the extra job.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'She ___ each pot slowly.'
options: water
watering
waters
watered

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: She cleans often the dust off the leaves.
Often she cleans the dust the leaves off it.
She often clean the dust off the leaves often.
She often cleans the dust off the leaves.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'often' goes before the main verb 'cleans', which agrees with 'she'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'often' go, and does 'she' need -s?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why she moves a plant?
options: Sometimes she moves a plant to a sunny corner because it looks weak.
Sometimes she moves a plant to a sunny corner because of it looks weak.
Sometimes she move a plant to a sunny corner because it look weak.
Sometimes she moves a plant to a sunny corner because weak it looks plant.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'moves' and 'looks' agree with singular subjects, and 'because' has a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb endings and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence contrasts being unnoticed with the result?
options: Most people never notices her, but the office is always green and fresh.
Most people never notice her, but the office is always green and fresh.
Most people never notice her, but the office are always green and fresh.
Most people notice never her, but always the office green and fresh is.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people' is plural ('notice', no -s), 'the office is' is singular, and 'but' shows contrast.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence says nobody pays her, with a contrast?
options: Nobody pay her for this, yet she do it every week.
Nobody pays her for this, yet she does it every weeks.
Nobody pays her for this, yet she does it every week.
Nobody pays her for this, yet every week it she does do.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Nobody' takes a singular verb ('pays'), and 'she does' is correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the plants growing most naturally?
options: The plants by the window grows tall, and one even have small white flowers now.
The plants by the window grow tall, and one even has small white flower now.
The plants by the window grow tall, and small white flowers one even has now it.
The plants by the window grow tall, and one even has small white flowers now.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'plants grow' (plural, no -s), 'one has' (singular -s), and 'flowers' is plural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes how you feel, naturally?
options: When I feel tired at my desk, I look at the green leaves and I feel calm.
When I feel tired at my desk, I looks at the green leaves and I feel calm.
When I feel tired at my desk, I look at the green leaves and I feel calmly.
When I feel tired at my desk, at the green leaves I look and calm I feel it.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the verb has no -s, and 'calm' is the correct adjective after 'feel'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb with 'I' and the word after 'feel'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states the small-but-important idea?
options: Her job is very small, but I think it make everyone here a little happier.
Her job is very small, but I think it makes everyone here a little happier.
Her job is very small, but I think it makes everyone here a little happy more.
Her job very small is, but everyone here it makes a little happier I think it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'it makes' agrees with 'it', and 'a little happier' is the natural comparative phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb 'makes' and the comparative form.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence reports the past event correctly?
options: Last week I finally say thank you, and she just smiled.
Last week I finally said thank you, and she just smile.
Last week I finally said thank you, and she just smiled.
Last week I finally said thank you, and she just smiling.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'said' and 'smiled' are both correct past simple forms for a finished event.
mcQuestions.hint: The event happened last week, so use the past.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do we often forget to thank the people who do small, quiet jobs around us, and who is one such person in your own life?
